prompt: Create a gritty retro-futurist deep-sea science fiction movie poster in a tall 2:3 theatrical one-sheet format, with a dark navy-black worn paper background, orange and tan ink, subtle vertical fold marks, scratches, halftone grain, barcode-like production marks, thin technical diagram lines, sonar contour waves, crosshair symbols, small star glints, and wireframe globe icons. At the very top place the tagline TRUST NO SYSTEM. QUESTION EVERYTHING. in small condensed tan uppercase lettering. Dominating the upper third, use huge jagged orange brush lettering for the title PARALLEL PROTOCOL, split around a small centered boxed credit reading “A FILM BY N.MART.” The poster features exactly 4 futuristic operatives standing shoulder-to-shoulder in dramatic low-angle lighting: left, a dark armored soldier with long hair, glossy black tactical suit and weapon harness; center-left, the lead woman in a black tailored blazer over a short black dress, lace thigh-high stockings, garters, gloves, braided updo, and a confident commanding stance; center-right, a figure in a flowing white deep-sea pressure coat with blue wave-like patterns, black breathing apparatus and cybernetic gear; right, a black armored female operative with orange illuminated weapon details and heavy tactical cables. Faces should be cinematic but unobscured, with moody rim lighting in burnt orange and cool blue. Add exactly 2 critic quotes: left quote “A VISUALLY STUNNING RIDE.” with attribution “— FILM PULSE,” and right quote “BOLD. STYLISH. UNFORGETTABLE.” with attribution “— INDIE WIRE.” Along the left margin include vertical studio text “N.MART STUDIOS” and the year “2025.” Near the lower character feet place exactly 4 cast names in small orange uppercase: “ELIOT VALE,” “MILES KORBEN,” “JULIAN HART,” and “LEO DRAKE.” In the lower center, add “IN THEATERS” above a huge tan release date 06.27.2025, followed by “EXPERIENCE IT IN IMAX AND REALD 3D,” with “IMAX” emphasized in orange. Finish with tiny production credits across the bottom, an “N.MART STUDIOS” logo box on the bottom left, a PG-13 rating box on the bottom right, and a small barcode strip centered near the bottom. Overall mood: stylish nonexistent 1980s future-cinema poster, deep-sea paranoia, cyberpunk espionage, premium illustrated-photoreal hybrid, high contrast, detailed fabric, dramatic shadows, aged print texture.
